---
title: The Last Byte
subtitle: '**From Raw Data to Well-Done Visualizations**'
author:
  - name: 'Kevin Madden <br> *Assistant Director of Annual Giving* <br> *Villanova University*'
    # email: <br> *kevin.madden@villanova.edu*
date: 15 July 2026
date-format: long
format:
    revealjs:
        embed-resources: true
        self-contained-math: true
        smaller: true
        scrollable: true
#        reference-location: document
        theme: dracula_custom.scss
bibliography: references.bib
---

## Before We Begin...
::: {style='text-align: center;'}
*What is your level of experience with data visualizations?*
:::

![](https://github.com/kfmadden/the-last-byte/blob/main/quiz1.png?raw=true){fig-align="center" height=20%}

## Opening Survey Results

<div class="strawpoll-embed" id="strawpoll_w4nWWpQXlnA" style="height: 708px; max-width: 640px; width: 100%; margin: 0 auto; display: flex; flex-direction: column;"><iframe title="StrawPoll Embed" id="strawpoll_iframe_w4nWWpQXlnA" src="https://strawpoll.com/embed/w4nWWpQXlnA/results" style="position: static; visibility: visible; display: block; width: 100%; flex-grow: 1;" frameborder="0" allowfullscreen allowtransparency>Loading...</iframe><script nonce="NWRhMzhjM2RjZWRlNzJiZWFlNzlhMzc1YTg5YmE1YmFlYWI0ZjUzMw==" async src="https://cdn.strawpoll.com/dist/widgets.js" charset="utf-8"></script></div>

## Session Overview

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width='45%'}
<u>What we *WON'T* focus on</u>

::: {.fragment}
- Data wrangling/cleaning/mining
  - [Cross Industry Standard Process for Data Mining (CRISP-DM)](https://www.datascience-pm.com/crisp-dm-2/)
  - [Extract-Transform-Load (ETL)](https://www.databricks.com/discover/etl)
:::
::: {.fragment}
- Programming and coding tutorials
  - [R](https://rstudio-education.github.io/hopr/) and [Python](https://nostarch.com/python-crash-course-3rd-edition)
  - [Power Query M formula language](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powerquery-m/)
  - [Data Analysis Expressions (DAX)](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dax/)
:::
:::

::: {.column width='10%'}
:::

::: {.column width='45%'}
<u>What we *WILL* focus on</u>

::: {.fragment}
- Data viz theory
  - Gestalt principles of visual perception
  - Preattentive processing
:::
::: {.fragment}
- Data viz best practices
  - What makes visualizations good (and not-so-good)
:::
::: {.fragment}
- Types of visualizations and their use cases
  - Advancement-specific examples
:::
:::

::::

## So...Why Visualizations?

Why not just stick to Excel sheets or tables with raw numbers?


## Gestalt Principles of Visual Perception

::: {style='text-align: center;'}
[<u>Gestalt</u> $\approx$ unified whole, configuration]{.highlight-orange}
:::

. . .

Our brains tend to group objects based on some of the following criteria:

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width='47%'}

::: {.fragment}
- **Proximity**
  - The objects are close to one another.
:::

::: {.fragment}
- **Similarity**
  - The objects share features, such as color, size, shape, or direction.
:::

::: {.fragment}
- **Enclosure**
  - There is a defined boundary surrounding the objects.
:::

:::

::: {.column width='6%'}
:::

::: {.column width='47%'}

::: {.fragment}
- **Closure**
  - Gaps between the objects are suggestive of a whole.
:::
::: {.fragment}
- **Continuity**
  - The objects align or continue in a discernible order.
:::
::: {.fragment}
- **Connection**
  - The objects are connected to each other.
:::

:::

::::

## Examples of Gestalt Principles

::: {.panel-tabset}

### Proximity
```{r proximity}
library(pacman)
p_load(ggplot2, dplyr)
set.seed(1217)

clust_A <- data.frame(
  x = rnorm(200, mean = 2, sd = 0.3),
  y = rnorm(200, mean = 2, sd = 0.3),
  group = 'A')

clust_B <- data.frame(
  x = rnorm(500, mean = 4, sd = 0.5),
  y = rnorm(500, mean = 4, sd = 0.5),
  group = 'B')

data <- rbind(clust_A, clust_B)

ggplot(data, aes(x = x, y = y)) +
  geom_point(color = '#ff79c6', alpha = 0.5, size = 3) +
  labs(title = 'Objects close to one another tend to be perceived as a group.') +
  theme_void() +
  theme(
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = '#282a36', color = NA),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = '#282a36', color = NA),
    plot.title = element_text(color = '#f8f8f2', hjust = 0.5, face = 'bold')
  )
```

### Similarity
```{r similarity}
ggplot(data, aes(x = x, y = y, color = group)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5, size = 3) +
  labs(title = 'Objects sharing similar attributes like color tend to be perceived as a group.') +
  scale_color_manual(values = c('#ffb86c', '#8be9fd')) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = '#282a36', color = NA),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = '#282a36', color = NA),
    plot.title = element_text(color = '#f8f8f2', hjust = 0.5, face = 'bold'),
    legend.position = 'none'
  )
```

### Enclosure
```{r enclosure}
p_load(ggforce)
ggplot(data, aes(x = x, y = y, color = group)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5, size = 3) +
  labs(title = 'Objects enclosed by a boundary tend to be perceived as a group.') +
  geom_mark_ellipse(aes(color = group)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c('#ffb86c', '#8be9fd')) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = '#282a36', color = NA),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = '#282a36', color = NA),
    plot.title = element_text(color = '#f8f8f2', hjust = 0.5, face = 'bold'),
    legend.position = 'none'
  )
```

### Closure
```{r closure}
set.seed(1217)
close_data_missing <- data.frame(x = c(1:30)) %>% 
  mutate(
    y = ifelse(
      x %in% c(20:25),
      NA,
      rnorm(100, mean = 2, sd = 1)
    )
  )

close_data_expected <- close_data_missing %>% 
  filter(
    x == 19 | x == 26
  )

close_data_actual <- close_data_missing %>% 
  mutate(
    y = ifelse(
      x == 19 | x == 26,
      close_data_missing$y,
      ifelse(
        x < 20 | x > 25,
        NA,
        rnorm(100, mean = 4, sd = 1)
      )
    )
  )

p1 <- ggplot(close_data_missing, aes(x = x, y = y)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1, color = '#ffb86c') +
  labs(title = 'Our brains tend to fill in the gaps between objects, completing suggested patterns.') +
  ylim(-1,8) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = '#282a36', color = NA),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = '#282a36', color = NA),
    plot.title = element_text(color = '#f8f8f2', hjust = 0.5, face = 'bold')
  )

p2 <- p1 +
  geom_line(data = close_data_expected, linewidth = 1, linetype = 'dashed', color = '#8be9fd')

p3 <- p2 +
  geom_line(data = close_data_actual, linewidth = 1, color = '#ffb86c', alpha = 0.5) +
  labs(subtitle = 'But sometimes, our brains are wrong!') +
  theme(
    plot.subtitle = element_text(color = '#f8f8f2', hjust = 0.5, face = 'bold.italic', size = 12)
  )
```

::: {.r-stack}
```{r closure-plot-1}
p1
```

::: {.fragment}
```{r closure-plot-2}
p2
```
:::

::: {.fragment}
```{r closure-plot-3}
p3
```
:::
:::

### Continuity
```{r continuity}
cont_data <- data.frame(
  x = c('A', 'B', 'C', 'D', 'E', 'F', 'G'),
  y = c(100, 150, 90, 180, 200, 70, 120)
)

p1 <- ggplot(cont_data, aes(x = x, y = y))+
  geom_bar(stat = 'identity', fill = '#ffb86c') +
  labs(title = 'Our eyes more readily perceive smooth and continuous paths, lines, and curves as connected forms.') +
  theme_void() +
  theme(
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = '#282a36', color = NA),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = '#282a36', color = NA),
    plot.title = element_text(color = '#f8f8f2', hjust = 0.5, face = 'bold')
  )

p2 <- ggplot(cont_data, aes(x = reorder(x, -y), y = y))+
  geom_bar(stat = 'identity', fill = '#8be9fd') +
  labs(title = 'Our eyes more readily perceive smooth and continuous paths, lines, and curves as connected forms.') +
  theme_void() +
  theme(
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = '#282a36', color = NA),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = '#282a36', color = NA),
    plot.title = element_text(color = '#f8f8f2', hjust = 0.5, face = 'bold')
  )

# (p1 | p2) +
#   plot_annotation(
#     title = 'Our eyes more readily perceive smooth and continuous paths, lines, and curves as connected forms.',
#     theme = theme(
#       plot.background = element_rect(fill = '#282a36', color = NA),
#       panel.background = element_rect(fill = '#282a36', color = NA),
#       plot.title = element_text(color = '#f8f8f2', hjust = 0.5, face = 'bold'))
#   )
```

::: {.r-stack}
```{r continuity-plot-1}
p1
```

::: {.fragment}
```{r continuity-plot-2}
p2
```
:::
:::

### Connection
```{r connection}
set.seed(1217)
connect_data <- data.frame(
  x = rep(1:50, 2),
  group = rep(c('A', 'B'), each = 50)) %>% 
    mutate(
      y = ifelse(
        group == 'A',
        rnorm(100, mean = 4, sd = 1),
        rnorm(100, mean = 2, sd = 1))
    )

p1 <- ggplot(connect_data, aes(x = x, y = y)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, color = '#ff79c6') +
  labs(title = 'Connected objects tend to be perceived as a group.') +
  ylim(-2,10) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = '#282a36', color = NA),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = '#282a36', color = NA),
    plot.title = element_text(color = '#f8f8f2', hjust = 0.5, face = 'bold'),
    legend.position = 'none'
  )

p2 <- ggplot(connect_data, aes(x = x, y = y, color = group)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  labs(title = 'Connected objects tend to be perceived as a group.') +
  scale_color_manual(values = c('#ffb86c', '#8be9fd')) +
  ylim(-2,10) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = '#282a36', color = NA),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = '#282a36', color = NA),
    plot.title = element_text(color = '#f8f8f2', hjust = 0.5, face = 'bold'),
    legend.position = 'none'
  )

p3 <- ggplot(connect_data, aes(x = x, y = y, color = group)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  labs(title = 'Connected objects tend to be perceived as a group.') +
  scale_color_manual(values = c('#ffb86c', '#8be9fd')) +
  ylim(-2,10) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = '#282a36', color = NA),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = '#282a36', color = NA),
    plot.title = element_text(color = '#f8f8f2', hjust = 0.5, face = 'bold'),
    legend.position = 'none'
  )

# (p1 / p2 / p3) +
#   plot_annotation(
#     title = 'Connected objects tend to be perceived as a group.',
#     theme = theme(
#       plot.background = element_rect(fill = '#282a36', color = NA),
#       panel.background = element_rect(fill = '#282a36', color = NA),
#       plot.title = element_text(color = '#f8f8f2', hjust = 0.5, face = 'bold'))
#   )
```

::: {.r-stack}
```{r connection-plot-1}
p1
```

::: {.fragment}
```{r connection-plot-2}
p2
```
:::

::: {.fragment}
```{r connection-plot-3}
p3
```
:::
:::

:::

## Cleaning Your Data
- Summarize it
- Transform it

## Exploring Your Data
- Get stats [@donor_data]
- Basic plots

## Visualizing Your Data
![](https://cup-us.imgix.net/covers/9780231193115.jpg?auto=format&w=350)

- This [book](https://cup.columbia.edu/book/better-data-visualizations/9780231193115/) by @schwabish is an extremely useful resource

## Pulse Check
::: {style='text-align: center;'}
*Which data viz tools have you used before?*
:::

![](https://github.com/kfmadden/the-last-byte/blob/main/quiz2.png?raw=true){fig-align="center" height=20%}

## Pulse Check Results

<div class="strawpoll-embed" id="strawpoll_61gD9NVvVZw" style="height: 708px; max-width: 640px; width: 100%; margin: 0 auto; display: flex; flex-direction: column;"><iframe title="StrawPoll Embed" id="strawpoll_iframe_61gD9NVvVZw" src="https://strawpoll.com/embed/61gD9NVvVZw" style="position: static; visibility: visible; display: block; width: 100%; flex-grow: 1;" frameborder="0" allowfullscreen allowtransparency>Loading...</iframe><script nonce="ZmZjYTJhNGYxMWQ0ZDMxNjJkNDMxZjM5N2IzNTU4NDRhOTgwMjBiNg==" async src="https://cdn.strawpoll.com/dist/widgets.js" charset="utf-8"></script></div>

## Code Blocks

``` {.python code-line-numbers="4-5|7|10"}
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

r = np.arange(0, 2, 0.01)
theta = 2 * np.pi * r
fig, ax = plt.subplots(subplot_kw={'projection': 'polar'})
ax.plot(theta, r)
ax.set_rticks([0.5, 1, 1.5, 2])
ax.grid(True)
plt.show()
```

## References
::: {refs}
:::
